children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Any person who violates shall be fined under this title, or imprisoned for any term of years, or both.section 229 of this title

children.heading: .—Death penalty
children.label: 2 .—Death penalty
children.text: Any person who violates and by whose action the death of another person is the result shall be punished by death or imprisoned for life.section 229 of this title

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Attorney General may bring a civil action in the appropriate United States district court against any person who violates and, upon proof of such violation by a preponderance of the evidence, such person shall be subject to pay a civil penalty in an amount not to exceed $100,000 for each such violation.section 229 of this title

children.heading: .—Relation to other proceedings
children.label: 2 .—Relation to other proceedings
children.text: The imposition of a civil penalty under this subsection does not preclude any other criminal or civil statutory, common law, or administrative remedy, which is available by law to the United States or any other person.

children.heading: .—Reimbursement of Costs
children.label: c .—Reimbursement of Costs
children.text: The court shall order any person convicted of an offense under subsection (a) to reimburse the United States for any expenses incurred by the United States incident to the seizure, storage, handling, transportation, and destruction or other disposition of any property that was seized in connection with an investigation of the commission of the offense by that person. A person ordered to reimburse the United States for expenses under this subsection shall be jointly and severally liable for such expenses with each other person, if any, who is ordered under this subsection to reimburse the United States for the same expenses.
